$comment: Example TWAK (Trust Wallet Agent Kit) signing policy for the Guardrail self-custody demo. This is illustrative configuration: it documents the authorization envelope the user's wallet enforces. Keys and signing authority NEVER leave the user's wallet — the agent only requests authorizations, and TWAK is the sole signer and execution venue. See docs/TWAK_SELF_CUSTODY_DEMO.md and docs/SELF_CUSTODY.md.
name: Guardrail TWAK Self-Custody Signing Policy (example)

custody.note: The agent builds OrderIntents and authorization payloads. Only TWAK, holding the user's keys, can sign or broadcast. execute_swap requires a risk-engine-minted ApprovedOrder, so signing without prior risk approval is structurally unrepresentable.

allowed_contracts.$comment: Only contracts listed here may be targeted by a signed authorization. The competition contract mirrors apps/guardrail-api/src/compete.rs; payment_token mirrors configs/x402/signing_policy.json.

execution.risk_gate: No RiskDecision::Approved means no TWAK swap
